title: Sóng Gió
license_note: User supplied this audio and lyric text for local evaluation; do not redistribute the source audio or lyric text.

sections.name: verse_1
sections.design: local rhyme pairs at the opening, then freer conversational lines

sections.name: chorus
sections.design: four semantic lines with oi/ôi vowel-family cohesion, followed by repeated vocables and call-and-response

sections.name: verse_2
sections.design: four-line assonance blocks, then repeated au/âu sounds, then phrase repetition

sections.name: bridge
sections.design: rhyme is secondary; emotional lift and longer sustained phrases

sections.name: outro
sections.design: story closure with varied endings, no forced pair rhyme

chorus_rules: Use one short anchor phrase twice in the main four-line chorus.
Prefer a four-line semantic hook before ad-libs or vocables.
Keep line endings in one loose vowel family when it is natural, but do not require every adjacent pair to match.
Reserve repeated vocables for a post-chorus or final-chorus lift.
verse_rules: Use local rhyme clusters of two to four lines, then allow a free line to preserve meaning.
Score vowel assonance separately from exact coda matches.
Preserve natural word order and speech-tone contours over a forced rhyme word.
timing_rules: For a 28-30 second backing, generate enough lyric material to span most of the arrangement.
Prefer comfortable syllable spacing and slower TTS speed over compressing many words into the first phrase.
